commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a reset-time backfill before storing Codex visible-account snapshots and adds focused regression coverage plus a test-helper argument for cached reset times.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main stores per-visible-account snapshots after applying the Codex visible-account label, while selected/global paths already backfill reset timestamps. I did not run live provider checks because AGENTS.md warns against live provider/keychain validation without an explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual follow-up is needed because the PR still needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a scoped account-identity repair before merge.

target.title: Preserve account reset timestamps
target.body:
Fixes #1283

## Summary
- Backfill known reset metadata before persisting per-account visible usage snapshots.
- Add a regression test that verifies the matching account receives the cached reset time while another visible account does not.

## Root cause
The selected/global refresh path already backfilled missing reset timestamps from the last known snapshot before updating state. The per-account visible refresh path labeled the account snapshot and stored it immediately, so a fresh response that omitted `resetsAt` could persist without the known 5-hour reset metadata.

## Proof
Provider proof feasibility: local CLI was installed and authenticated. A redacted live `--all-accounts` refresh returned reset metadata through the installed account path:

```json
{
  "source": "codex-cli",
  "usage": {
    "accountEmail": "[redacted-email]",
    "primary": {
      "resetsAt": "2026-06-04T08:32:42Z",
      "usedPercent": 43,
      "windowMinutes": 300
    },
    "secondary": {
      "resetsAt": "2026-06-11T03:32:42Z",
      "usedPercent": 7,
      "windowMinutes": 10080
    }
  }
}
```

Regression coverage proves the stored account snapshot now preserves the known reset value for the matching account and does not copy it to a different visible account.

## Checks
- `make check`
- `swift build`
- `swift test --filter CodexAccountScopedRefreshTests`
- `swift test --filter ResetTimeBackfillTests`
- `swift test --filter UsageStorePlanUtilizationCodexOwnershipTests`
- `./.build/debug/CodexBarCLI usage --provider codex --all-accounts --source cli --format json --pretty --json-only` with emails redacted

I also ran the full `swift test`; it completed with one unrelated existing AppKit/menu timing failure in `StatusMenuOpenRefreshTests.swift:208` (`closed attached menu is prepared before next open after invalidation`, menu version 2 vs 3). The touched files are limited to account snapshot backfill and tests.

## Reviews
- Two local review gates completed with no blocker findings.

Remaining risk: proof is bounded to the local authenticated CLI/account path plus focused state tests; the full suite still has the unrelated menu timing flake noted above.
target.author: rohitjavvadi
